reasoning: The time series tool provides a combined statistical estimate of P(>29.09) = 0.44, driven by the strong downward momentum over the last 5 data points and historical seasonal weakness. HPQ saw a sharp ~7% drop leading into Feb 28 following its Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings report, which disappointed on Q2 guidance and highlighted weak short-term prospects despite a raised full-year outlook. This indicates short-term downward pressure consistent with post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD). Therefore, I anchor the initial probability for the nearest 1-week horizon (March 9) at the tool's 0.44 estimate. As the forecast horizon extends to 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, and 1 year, the short-term momentum effects and predictability of PEAD diminish, increasingly outweighed by random market noise. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase and distant dates must pull closer to 0.5, I gradually regress the probability predictions upward along a smooth curve from 0.44 up to 0.49 for the 1-year mark.
